methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $25M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $300M - $10.0B.

private.name: 1X Technologies
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $10B

private.name: Bedrock Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $1.75B

private.name: Dexterity
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $1.65B

private.name: Collaborative Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $500M+

private.name: Fairmat
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $350M

private.name: GrayMatter Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $300M+

data.narrative:
Recycleye, a Series B robotics and manufacturing company founded by Victor Dewulf, has raised $25M+ to date. Valuation benchmarking draws on a peer set of 9 comparable firms (1 public, 8 private) operating in the same sector and stage, including 1X Technologies, Bedrock Robotics, Dexterity, Collaborative Robotics, and Fairmat.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis uses the $25M total raised as a valuation proxy, applying a 1.9x multiplier typical of Series B-to-next-stage advancement, yielding an implied valuation of $48M. Peer-median analysis references six same-sector, same-stage comparables with a median valuation of $1.1B, though the peer distribution is wide, ranging from $300M to $10.0B. This dispersion reflects meaningful heterogeneity in market positioning and capital intensity within frontier robotics.

The triangulated analysis produces a range of $40M (low, weighted toward stage-progression) to $1.2B (high, weighted toward peer median), with a median midpoint of $561M. The wide spread reflects structural uncertainty: stage-progression relies on historical raise multiples and assumes Recycleye will track peer advancement rates, while peer-median valuation carries execution and market-timing risk given the volatility observed across the comparable set. Key uncertainties include unit economics maturity, revenue trajectory relative to capital intensity, and sector adoption rates in waste management automation.
